page: Microsoft Azure D16pds_v5 is an ARM64-based virtual machine belonging to the DPDSv5 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 16 dedicated physical cores powered by an Ampere Neoverse-N1 processor operating at 3.0 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 64.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. For local storage, the instance is equipped with a 614 GB local disk and supports Premium Storage. There are no onboard GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses included with this configuration.

Performance benchmarks place the D16pds_v5 in the average tier for most multi-core and memory operations. The instance achieved a stress-ng multi-core CPU score of 28887.7 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 9559.0. Memory bandwidth is robust, reaching 1125.92 GB/sec for cached small-block reads and 129.87 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads. However, single-core performance is a relative bottleneck, with Geekbench single-core scoring in the lower 25% at 1108.0, and single-threaded bzip3 decompression also registering in the weak tier. Cryptographic performance is limited, as evidenced by a weak Passmark CPU encryption score of 2591.19, which correlates with the lack of advanced vector or matrix extensions.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the ARM64 architecture offers a resource-dense profile that delivers steady multi-threaded performance and high memory bandwidth without the premium associated with traditional x86 architectures. This makes the D16pds_v5 highly suitable for scale-out workloads that benefit from dedicated physical cores and local storage. Optimal workloads include static web serving, where it achieves a throughput of 17.95 GB/sec, database operations, Redis deployments measuring 22.06 million SET operations per second, and multi-threaded software compilation. It is less suited for single-threaded tasks or heavy cryptographic operations.
description: The Microsoft Azure D16pds_v5 is an ARM64-based virtual machine from the DPDSv5 family, powered by a 16-core Ampere Neoverse-N1 processor running at 3.0 GHz. It features 64.0 GB of RAM and 614 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability. Operating under Microsoft Hyper-V, this instance provides dedicated CPU allocation with no hyperthreading. Benchmark data indicates average multi-core and memory bandwidth performance, though single-core and cryptographic execution speeds are relatively weak. With a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and high multi-threaded efficiency, the D16pds_v5 is well-suited for general-purpose workloads, static web serving, Redis caching, and database operations that benefit from local storage and dedicated physical cores.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D16pds_v5 is an ARM64 virtual machine featuring 16 dedicated Ampere Neoverse-N1 cores, 64 GB RAM, and 614 GB local storage, delivering balanced multi-core performance for general-purpose workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D16pds_v5 is an ARM64 virtual machine with 16 dedicated Ampere cores, 64 GB RAM, and 614 GB local storage for general-purpose workloads.
tagline: An ARM64-powered virtual machine featuring dedicated physical cores, balanced memory, and local storage for efficient multi-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: 16 dedicated Ampere Neoverse-N1 ARM64 physical cores running at 3.0 GHz
64.0 GB of system memory providing a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio
614 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Strong multi-threaded performance for database operations and Redis caching
Lower single-core and cryptographic performance relative to multi-core capabilities
